I did a lot of research on windshield sunshades looking for one I could live with for a while. All of the ones I liked the most, unfortunately, cost more than I wanted to pay. Ultimately I settled for this one because it is almost identical to one I had for about five years, except that one was silver on both sides. This one is silver on one side and has a lovely pastel pattern on the other--which is the side I display. It folds up in an accordion and has a strap that goes around it and fastens with Velcro to keep it neatly stored away when not in use while driving. This product works as promised to keep sun out of the car and noticeably lowers the temperature. It also protects the vinyl dashboard and seats.

I originally reviewed this product as 5 stars. I am downgrading it to 2 stars because after only 2 months it is starting to fall apart, shedding little silver flakes everywhere. That silver snow is why I got rid of my last sunshade, but it took that one years to get in that shape, not months. I am very disappointed with this product.

1. Cheaply made, but maybe it will be fine, right? wrong. It's cheaply made. The "on-off" button's plastic "weatherproof" covering came off immediately. I don't even want to put it on the house, knowing the first sprinkle we have the thing will be toast.
2. 2 lights with 5 LEDs each should be super bright, right? wrong. It lights up a 30 sq ft area dimly. I'd say if you installed this no more than 8 ft up and both lights pointing straight down, you'd have a 4ft area dimly lit.

What do we like? There is LOTS of versatility with the way in which you can arrange the light, solar panel, etc. Everything on it seems to telescope, rotate, etc. It comes with hardware to install in your garden or on a wall.
Based on the brightness, if you wanted to light up your rose bushes or ivy in your garden, this would do great...but you can do it more cheaply than this.

We wanted to use this to light up part of our driveway, if even dimly, but this is NOT the product for that...it looks powerful, it looks like it will do a good job, but it doesn't. Bummer...

This product "foams" when sprayed, and it seems to require quite a bit of product.  It is also slightly aggressive but approved for use on most (if not all?) factory OEM wheels.  It did however work well on the 8 wheels I cleaned.  Each had to be rinsed first, sprayed, the while waiting for 30 seconds I scrubbed each wheel and inner surface lightly with a brush, and then rinsed the product off.  This process worked well for me, the product did not work well on it's own without scrubbing, but it did not take much effort.  This bottle might only last for 4-6 cars, it seems to be used quickly for good coverage of both wheel and tire sidewalls.

This is what it is. It works well and comes with the tube to help pour it safely and cleanly. The only difference between this and the manufacturer version is that the OEM filler tube fits snug and the flow stops when the tank is full. With this version, it comes with a filler tube, but it's universal. This means when you go to fill the tank, you can easily overflow it if you hit full.

Personally, mine alerts at 1/2 tank with is 2.5 gallons. I wait until it alarms 1/2 level before filling and I can always get a full one in when this happens, without any fear of overflowing the tank.

I have a 05 TJ Unlimited. They say it will not work with a body lift installed. Fine, I removed my body lift and installed this. The shift throw was much shorter, but it hit the shifter trim. Not to mention the quality of shift was horrible. Where my OEM shifter was loose and perhaps a bit sloppy, this was firm to the point of being ridiculous. shifting into 1st and 2nd felt like it was shifting over sandpaper and loose gravel. The shifter itself is beautifully made. I called Holley's technical line and emailed them pictures of where it was hitting. Crickets...

I just cant believe that they would make such a precision shifter that 1) can't be used with a body lift (which probably near 100% of people considering this upgrade, already have and 2) appears to have limited post production testing.

I removed this shifter, reinstalled my oem (and body lift) and everything is back to normal again.

I am so disappointed, I have drooling over this for months as an upgrade and it fell completely flat

Full disclosure- this is a product installed on the exterior and controlled from the interior!  That means to succeed you will have to breach the fire wall (of the chassis) to wire properly. The remote receiver needs to be in the cabin and the lights on the front (patched into the engine bay). Expect to cut and route wires, fish lines through access holes (if they exist) and get dirty (or plan to budget $250-$500 for a pro install).  You better really love working on your car before buying. Lights are bright and work well but don’t underestimate the LOE on this project.

In the end, this product does what it was intended to do. I gave it 3 stars, rather than 5, because the first received was not operational. Amazon's fast replacement saved the day and we had a working unit. We installed this using the included documentation and specifics from the internet. If you gather all of the required documentation, installation is pretty straight forward. It took us about 2 hours to install this and have it up and running.

The range is decent, but not as good as expected. It will work from in the house to an attached driveway, but perhaps not from an office to a nearby parking lot. If you're looking for long range, this is probably not the right unit. If you're generally pretty close, this will work great and has a lot of features.

Also note that installation isn't plug and play. You do have to tap into wires and have some mechanical knowledge to install.

I would have given it 4 starts if the first unit hadn't arrived dead or if Bulldog's support line had been a little more responsive.

Well, like everything these days you're choices for buying something is from China or China. I've had the older body style of GM truck mirror for the GMT900 bodies and either it was better quality or I just happened to get a decent one other than the folding part broke leaving the mirror flopping around. This version (its the new body 2013 to 2018 GM trucks) suffers from the usual problems of cheap Chinese made junk truck mirrors. The mirrors shake far to much at highway speed to use them effectively. I should be clear the mirror housings don't shake nor do the convex "spot" mirrors, only the adjustable mirrors bounce around like mad. The right side does not fit as tightly on the sleeved arms as the driver's side. This causes the right side to slowly slide out from wind pressure and road vibration. I'm positive these are all made in the same or same two or three factories and are simply resold by every Tom Dick and Harry (would that be Lee, Wang and Wong over there?) under 50 million different company names. The plastic does not even seem to be as solid as the last pair and I suspect the Arizona sun will destroy them in a few months both with sun fade and physical degradation of the plastic itself. Not worth removing and trying to return, although Ideally I'd like my money back or the mirrors replaced.

I forgot to mention the wiring harness is so flipping short it's nearly impossible to follow the factory wiring channel to the switch pack. Another example of being cheap.

Hello ,
Over the past several months I had the brakes and rotors replaced on two cars both front and back using your products. I was extremely pleased with the product and decided to have the brakes on my 2000 CLK replaced as well. So I took the liberty to order the front pads and rotors from Amazon as I did in the past.
To my surprise and disappointment when the mechanic called me after I delivered the package to him he explained the package the pads came in and the rotors had been previously opened. He noted both pads and rotors had grease on them. He was not overly worried however he did explain sanding down the pads and using solvent to clean the rotors would solve the problem.
Since he already had the car prepared for the installation I went along with his suggestions and he completed the work. The car has been returned to me and the equipment works as expected without any known flaws.
The delivery is not what I expected and I am disappointed that I would receive a product that has either already been used and placed back in the package or some other explanation I cannot account for.
After contacting the manufacture they assured me the package left their warehouse in new condition. They also believe if any discrepancies exist the most likely source may have been a return to Amazon by another customer and the package thus being shipped to me.
Regards
James Radle

My experience of this specific product, as received, was that it was an un-named knockoff. The molding of the plastic around the metal portions of the fuse had no markings or identification. Visually, quality looked to be adequate, actual testing of fuses was not performed. There were no means of verifying quality, testing, or certification from the manufacturer.

Knockoff, generic fuses can be unsafe due to lack of testing and/or regulation. A quick search can bring up issues with fuses not performing as intended and/or being recalled in the past.

So, this fits all the GM trucks from 99 to 2010.  I ordered the long length for the LTZ interior (remote start).  The length is correct from the latch to the door release. HOWEVER the plastic snap in retainer for the door lock is cast just slightly smaller than the OEM piece. As such the lock tabs that push in and spread out to secure the retainer don't do this and the cable pulls right out of the bracket.  I solved the issue by using a zip tie to keep the "fingers" spread and the retainer locked in place.  This is a manufacturing flaw, typical of China made crap. They get it close to OEM but not quite right.

First off I recommend using a small flat head screwdriver with the install. let’s start off by taking the factory brake pad off, just grab a corner and pull, poof it’s off, grab your new brake pedal pad, put the top of the rubber gasket over the top of the pedal and pull down. Once the top is in the gasket take your screwdriver and go from just under the top right corner  and guide the gasket alone the right side of the pedal, now repeat on the left side, then finally do the bottom, run your fingers along the entire perimeter of the back of the pedal to ensure it is completely seated. Last thing you want is the pedal cover to come off in a hard breaking situation. If yes move on to the gas pedal if no use the screwdriver and repeat the side or bottom that isn’t seated. GAS PEDAL: DO NOT REMOVE THE FACTORY PEDAL COVER ON THIS SIDE. I started with the top right corner, then moved down the right side “since there’s not really room for the screwdriver trick on that side” get the entire top right corner/right side under the gasket. Then move to the bottom, use screwdriver as needed. Then pop the gasket over the bottom left side and move upwards towards the last corner. Again run your fingers along the gasket and make sure it’s properly seated around the gas pedal, if not use your screwdriver to correct it. THIS ALL SOUNDS RELATIVELY SIMPLE BUT IT DID TAKE ME A LITTLE WHILE TO FIGURE IT OUT AND TO PROPERLY SEAT THE GASKET

I'm crazy about frogs, and my home and autos are decked out in frog-themed accessories and collectibles. I purchased these in 2013 so I've had ample time to test these out, and this is my assessment after significant use:

Pros

*Cute tree frog designs compliments my rearview mirror charm and matches the customized tree frog license plate border I own
*I'm self-employed working from home, and other than bi-annual road trips, the mats haven't been used nearly as often as they'd be by the average commuter in my area (hence the reason they still look pretty decent)
*Semi-decent quality and good price at time of purchase justified the splurge, but the asking price now is exorbitant
*Frogs are still nearly the same color as when I purchased them without fading, but this is only due to keeping my car in a garage from the sun's rays

Cons

*Flimsy, cheaply made mats minutely offset by the cuteness of the frog designs
*Were I a frequent commuter, these mats certainly would have shredded and needed replacing within a short timeframe
*Most annoying feature is the mats never stay in place, because the bottoms are cheaply made and the texture intended to anchor them don't adhere properly to my auto carpet
*Although I love frogs, these aren't an overall savvy consumer splurge when most people commute at least five days on an average work week

The frog mats are cute, but this feature doesn't negate unreasonable pricing, semi-decent quality, and overall cheapness. I noticed some people loved the mats, and it's easy to see why when viewing photos, but as a professional reviewer with the best interest of fellow consumers, I simply don't recommend them.

Install was easy to follow using the included instructions. Install on a 2019 Honda Accord (Hybrid) involves removing the mudflaps (if equipped, not part of instructions), the bumper cover, and then the rear impact bar. Installation is reverse of removal, with the hitch installed between the impact bar and vehicle. The bumper cover did require slight trimming, following the dimensions in the instructions it worked perfectly.

The hitch itself is very straightforward, a few pieces of metal, some tubing, etc, all welded together as expected. Unfortunately, the product quality is lacking in the actual finishing of the product. It appears to have a coating of powdercoat or similar paint over raw metal. The raw metal likely was not cleaned, etched, or otherwise processed, as the paint was chipped and had flaked off in a few spots. I do not expect this to remain rust free for more than 6mo. I expect a majority of the product to show significant rust after 2-3 years, and we will probably just replace the entire thing in 3-4 years.

AS Amazon apparently can't keep Upol Raptor Liner in stock, I tried this product. This is really a rubberized undercoating and not hard, bed lining product as is claimed. I use the aerosol cans of "truck bed coating" to refinish faded plastic trim parts. Surface are was properly prepped by using de-greaser, washing with soap and water, drying, scuffed with a scotch brite pad (shouldnt be needed as it's textured plastic) and I used Dupli-color plastic adhesion promoter.

The results are not even close to a hard bed liner. Applied on a hot low humidity day (10% or less in the desert) and left to cure for several days and the finish is soft and rubbery. I can indent it with a finger nail. The final stop coat left a granular texture that rubs off into fine particles. This was applied following the prep and application directions of several light coats 10 minutes or less part.

I am thoroughly dissatisfied with this product, do not recommend using it as anything other than rubberized undercoating.

There are a lot of quick-car-wax products on the market. Most of them are spray bottles of wax product that you spray on your car, then wipe/buff off with your own towels. This product has the towel built in so you don't have to provide your own and wash it afterwards.  None of these quick-wax product work as well as a good paste wax (don't look as good or last as long), but they do apply much more quickly. This Armor All quick-wax gives us decent results even without any buffing afterwards (always the most time consuming part of using a paste car wax). The instructions say you should buff with a separate towel on dark colored cars or if streaking is noticeable and ugly. If you're too lazy to wax your car the old fashioned way but still want it to look reasonably nice, this quick wax is a good alternative.

so after doing a total rebuild on this F150 spending $1750 on dozens of new parts as well as sending the heads out for a rebuilding. Once I got everything completely back together (2weeks) I kept having a misfire and the engine would shake under 1500 RPM. I started replacing various parts trying to solve the problem but nothing worked. I bought an engine scanner and it kept saying misfire 3 and 4. nothing i did fixed it. switching out plugs and coils (bought new on amazon too by the way) I finally broke down after 3 weeks never finding this mysterious ghost/demon issue so I took it to a shop. they looked at it for 2 days and then told me that all the fuel injectors are incorrect and not working for this truck. They started replacing the fuel injectors with ones that they got from the Ford dealership which obviously cost a lot more but they work. Lesson learned cheap ain’t good and good ain’t cheap. I will thank Amazon for the full refund. Have a blessed day folks enjoy your rebuild

I really teetering on the brink of giving it 2 stars but I'm giving it three stars because I think the problem is partly my fault. I found all the components of the set to be very sturdy and I like having the translucent elbow. I've had set up for quite some time and it holds up very well.

So what's my problem? Although I've tried to be very conscientious about screwing the fittings onto the hose, I've had several catastrophic failures! In the immortal words of Forrest Gump, that's all I'm going to say about that right now. Even though the ends of the fittings rotate where they connect to the outlets, the hose works itself loose somehow, requiring more vigilance than I like to engage in with poop containing pipes.

Also, after a couple years of use the fittings themselves began to leak. I understand that you can get new O-rings for them to remedy this, but I decided to let this go and try a new system instead. I selected the "Dominator" brand, based solely on the aspect of the fittings being permanently connected to the hose. Not only is this true but if they ever decided to come loose, which doesn't look likely, you could clearly see where that was happening and not be 'surprised'. It has the same rigid construction as the rhino flex hose, it appears just as heavy duty. I will miss having the clear elbow, but of course the small fitting of that sort could be added.

If my review prevents just one unfortunate "mudslide", my work is done.
